title.no-sidewalk: Should there be a No Sidewalk label here?

curb-ramp.no-button-2: This is a sidewalk to driveway transition
curb-ramp.no-button-2-tooltip: Sidewalk to driveway transitions are not curb ramps unless a dedicated ramp has been added
curb-ramp.no-button-3: This should be a Missing Curb Ramp label
curb-ramp.no-button-3-tooltip: Sometimes a missing curb ramp is accidentally mislabeled as a curb ramp

no-curb-ramp.no-button-3: This should be a Curb Ramp label
no-curb-ramp.no-button-3-tooltip: Sometimes a curb ramp is mislabeled as a missing ramp, often because it's missing a tactile warning

obstacle.no-button-1: This is not on a pedestrian path
obstacle.no-button-1-tooltip: If there is no sidewalk or the object is not on the part of the sidewalk meant for pedestrians, like in the image below, an Obstacle label is not needed
obstacle.no-button-2: There is ample space to avoid this object
obstacle.no-button-2-tooltip: On very wide sidewalks, easily avoidable objects should not be labeled as obstacles
obstacle.no-button-3: This should be a Surface Problem label
obstacle.no-button-3-tooltip: Grass and debris like leaves should be marked with a Surface Problem label

surface-problem.no-button-1: This should be a No Sidewalk label
surface-problem.no-button-1-tooltip: Gravel or grass are not considered a sidewalk, and should be marked with a No Sidewalk label
surface-problem.no-button-2: This is not on a pedestrian path
surface-problem.no-button-2-tooltip: If a surface problem is not on a path meant for pedestrians, it should not be labeled as a surface problem

no-sidewalk.no-button-3: This should be marked with a different label type
no-sidewalk.no-button-3-tooltip: Surface problems or obstacles may be mistakenly labeled as No Sidewalk

crosswalk.no-button-1: There is no visible crosswalk here
crosswalk.no-button-2: This is a stop line for cars

signal.no-button-1: This is only a traffic light for vehicles
signal.no-button-2: This is a sign with no light to indicate safe crossing
signal.no-button-3: This is a pole with no pedestrian signal or button

common.reason-1-tooltip: This could be because the image is blurry, what's being labeled is too far away, or a different angle is needed
common.reason-2-tooltip: Sometimes what's being labeled is visible but the label appears far away from it

no-curb-ramp.unsure-button-3: Not sure if a curb ramp is required
no-curb-ramp.unsure-button-3-tooltip: It may be unclear whether pedestrians should be able to cross at an intersection with a busier road

obstacle.unsure-button-3:
There might be enough space
to avoid the object
obstacle.unsure-button-3-tooltip: The width of the sidewalk may be difficult to judge from a picture

surface-problem.unsure-button-3: Might be too minor to label
surface-problem.unsure-button-3-tooltip: It may be unclear whether an issue like grass between sidewalk blocks needs to be labeled

no-sidewalk.unsure-button-3: Not sure if there should be a sidewalk here
no-sidewalk.unsure-button-3-tooltip: Sometimes more context is necessary, and it's hard to determine if a sidewalk is necessary from just one image

mission-start.title: Validate {{n}} {{label_type}} labels
mission-start.body: Your mission is to determine the correctness of __LABELCOUNT_PLACEHOLDER__ __LABELTYPE_PLACEHOLDER__ labels placed by other users!

mission-complete.title: Great Job!
mission-complete.body-1: You just validated {{n}} Curb Ramp labels.
mission-complete.body-2: You just validated {{n}} Missing Curb Ramp labels.
mission-complete.body-4: You just validated {{n}} Surface Problem labels.
mission-complete.body-7: You just validated {{n}} No Sidewalk labels.
mission-complete.body-9: You just validated {{n}} Marked Crosswalk labels.
mission-complete.body-10: You just validated {{n}} Pedestrian Signal labels.
mission-complete.no-new-mission-body: Click the button to start exploring.

mobile.no-new-mission-body: You can add more labels using a laptop/desktop computer, or you can validate labels in another city!
